title: Table of Contents
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Abbreviated titles of prints arranged by season: two boxes at top are spring, fan-shaped box is summer, bottom right box autumn and bottom left are winter prints

title: Nihonbashi: Clearing after Snow (日本橋雪晴, Nihonbashi yukibare)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: Kasumigaseki (霞がせき)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Street (today Kasumigaseki-zaka), guardhouse of residence of Asano, barracks of samurai of the Kuroda clan, Edo Bay
remarks: Kadomatsu gate pines and manzai dancers indicate a setting around New Year; kite with Japanese character for fish (Jap. "sakana") is a reference to the publisher of the series, Sakanaya Eikichi

title: Hibiya and Soto-Sakurada from Yamashita-chō (山下町日比谷外さくら田, Yamashita-chō Hibiya Soto-Sakurada)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Residence of daimyō Nabeshima Kansō from the Saga Domain, outer moat of Edo Castle, Mount Fuji
remarks: Kadomatsu pine, hagoita and kites indicate a setting around New year

title: Tsukudajima and Eitai Bridge (永代橋佃しま, Eitaibashi Tsukudajima)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Eitai Bridge, Sumida River, fishing boats of Tsukudajima
remarks: An almost identical composition titled Eitaibashi by Hiroshige exists in the second volume of his "Illustrated Souvenirs of Edo"

title: Ekōin Temple in Ryōgoku and Moto-Yanagi Bridge (両ごく回向院元柳橋, Ryōgoku Ekōin Moto-Yanagibashi)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Drum tower of Ekō-in, Honjo neighbourhood, Sumida River, residence of Matsudaira feudal lor of Tanba, Mount Fuji
remarks: Drum tower was associated with sumo tournaments held at Ekō-in; for marketing purposes the print appeared two months after a popular major exhibition of temple treasures at Ekō-in

title: Hatsune Riding Ground in Bakuro-chō (馬喰町初音の馬場, Bakuro-chō Hatsune no baba)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Hatsune Riding Ground and district's fire watch tower
remarks: Dyers of Konya-chō district using riding grounds to dry their cloths

title: Shops with Cotton Goods in Ōdenma-chō (大てんま町木綿店, Ōtenma-chō momendana)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Street scene with geishas, cotton shops in Ōdenma-chō

title: Suruga-chō (する賀てふ, Suruga tefu)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Mitsui (yagō: Echigoya) textile stores, Mount Fuji
remarks: Echigoya later became the Mitsukoshi department store chain whose modern day headquarters are located on the left side of the street in the print

title: Yatsukōji, Inside Sujikai Gate (筋違内八ツ小路, Sujikai uchi Yatsukōji)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Yatsukōji junction, Kanda River, Kanda Shrine
remarks: One of the few open spaces in Edo, created as fire-breaks

title: Sunrise at Kanda Myōjin Shrine (神田明神曙之景, Kanda Myōjin akebono no kei)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Probably produced as a souvenir of the shrine festival in memory of the Battle of Sekigahara celebrated on the 15th day of the 9th month

title: Kiyomizu Hall and Shinobazu Pond at Ueno (上野清水堂不忍ノ池, Ueno Kiyomizu-dō Shinobazu no ike)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Kiyomizu Hall of Kan'ei-ji, Moon Pine, Shinobazu Pond
remarks: Depicted pines are in reality much smaller

title: Ueno Yamashita (上野山した)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Published in the month after Hiroshige's death; composition probably based on his sketches but probably completed by Hiroshige II

title: Shitaya Hirokōji (下谷広小路)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Hirokōji ("Broadway"), premises of textile retailer Matsuzakaya
remarks: Published in the same month in which the depicted Matsuzakaya store re-opened after the 1855 earthquake

title: Temple Gardens in Nippori (日暮里寺院の林泉, Nippori jiin no rinsen)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Depicts cherries and azaleas in bloom while in reality they bloom at different times of the year

title: Suwa Bluff in Nippori (日暮里諏訪の台, Nippori Suwanodai)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Suwa Myōjin Shrine grounds, Mount Tsukuba

title: Flower Park and Dangozaka Slope in Sendagi (千駄木団子坂花屋敷, Sendagi Dangozaka Hanayashiki)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Cherry orchard, Pavilion of the Violet Spring (Shisentei)
remarks: The print is a reference to the reconstruction of the Shisentai after the 1855 earthquake and to Tokugawa Iesada's visit to the park two months prior to publication

title: View to the North from Asukayama (飛鳥山北の眺望, Asukayama kita no chōbō)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Possibly inspired by Tokugawa Iesada's visit to the park two months prior to publication

title: The Ōji Inari Shrine (王子稲荷の社, Ōji Inari no yashiro)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: Dam on the Otonashi River at Ōji, known as "The Great Waterfall" (王子音無川堰棣 世俗大瀧ト唱, Ōji Otonashigawa entei, sezoku Ōtaki to tonau)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Otonashi River, Amida Hall of Kinrin-ji temple, Takata-chō
remarks: Likely created to mark Tokugawa Iesada's visit to Kinrin-ji (Ōji Shrine) and Takata on the 21st day of the first month (also see no. 115, 116)

title: The Kawaguchi Ferry and Zenkōji temple (川口のわたし善光寺, Kawaguchi no watashi Zenkōji)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: A year after Hiroshige designed the print, the Buddha in the Amida Hall at the top was due to be exhibited publicly for the first time in 13 years; in a pun Hiroshige covered the hall of this  "secret Buddha" with the title cartouche

title: Mount Atago in Shiba (芝愛宕山, Shiba Atagoyama)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Scene depicts an emissary from Enpuku-ji temple who on every third day of the year performs a ceremony at Atago Shrine for good fortune, health and success and to avert hunger and disease; the large rice paddle in his hand symbolizes abundance, the seaweed around his neck was distributed after the ceremony among the faithful who used it to brew an infusion against colds

title: Hiroo on Furukawa River (広尾ふる川, Hiroo Furukawa)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: Chiyogaike Pond in Meguro (目黒千代か池川, Meguro Chiyogaike)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Feature a — for the time — unusual depiction of reflections of the trees in the water

title: New Fuji in Meguro (目黒新富士, Meguro Shin-Fuji)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Mita Aqueduct, Mount Fuji replica, Mount Fuji
remarks: Subject is a replica (one of many) of Mount Fuji erected in 1829 by Fuji worshippers

title: The Original Fuji in Meguro (目黒元不二, Meguro Moto-Fuji)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Fuji replica erected in 1812 was at 12 m (39 ft) the tallest in Edo; this print was incorrectly placed in the spring section of the table of contents since the depicted cherry trees are in autumn foliage; spelling of "Fuji" in the title as (不二; lit. "not two") is thought to be a pun on the doubling of the mountain

title: The "Armour-Hanging Pine" at Hakkeizaka Bluff (八景坂鎧掛松, Hakkeizaka Yoroikakematsu)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Depicts a popular viewpoint of Edo Bay; according to legend, Minamoto no Yoshiie hung his armour on this tree in 1062; Hiroshige adapts the shape of the tree so that only a giant could have hung his armour on it

title: Plum Orchard in Kamada (蒲田の梅園, Kamada no umezono)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: Palace Hill in Shinagawa (品川御殿やま, Shinagawa Gotenyama)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Depicts both a famous cherry blossom spot and the destruction of the landscape after removal of earth; the earth was used to build ramparts (daiba) in the sea following Commodore Perry's arrival in Edo Bay in 1853

title: Moto-Hachiman Shrine in Sunamura (砂むら元八まん, Sunamura Moto-Hachiman)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Torii of Moto-Hachiman Shrine, Edo Bay

title: Plum Park in Kameido (亀戸梅屋舗, Kameido Umeyashiki)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Both in color and theme this print is related to print no. 27 (Plum Orchard in Kamada); copied by Vincent van Gogh under the title Japonaiserie: Flowering Plum Tree

title: Azuma no mori Shrine and the Entwined Camphor (吾嬬の森連理の梓, Azuma no mori Renri no azusa)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: The Yanagishima (柳しま, Yanagishima)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Myōken Hall of Hōshō-ji temple, Hashimotoya restaurant ("At the Foot of the Bridge"), Mount Tsukuba

title: Towboats Along the Yotsugi-dōri Canal (四ツ木通用水引ふね, Yotsugi dōri yōsui hikifune)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Unlike in the print, the canal and towpath were absolutely straight

title: Night View of Matsuchiyama and the San'ya Canal (真乳山山谷堀夜景, Matsuchiyama San'yabori yakei)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Sumida River, San'ya Canal inlet, Yumeiro and Takeya restaurants, Matsuchiyama hill, Shōten Shrine
remarks: Only image in the series to depict a large human figure, purportedly the favourite geisha of Hiroshige; Shōten Shrine on the top of the hill has sexual connotations

title: Suijin Shrine and Massaki on the Sumida River (隅田川水神の森真崎, Sumidagawa Suijin no mori Massaki)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Cherry tree trunk, Suijin Shrine, Sumida River, Massaki district, Mount Tsukuba
remarks: People at the bottom of the print are on the way to the Hashiba ferry (see no. 37)

title: View From Massaki of Suijin Shrine, Uchigawa Inlet, and Sekiya (真崎辺より水神の森内川関屋の里を見る図, Massaki atari yori Suijin no mori Uchigawa Sekiya no sato wo miru zu)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Sumida River, Suijin Shrine, Massaki district, Mount Tsukuba
remarks: Similar subject and location as no. 35

title: Kilns and the Hashiba Ferry on the Sumida River (墨田河橋場の渡かわら竈, Sumidagawa hashiba no watashi kawaragama)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Kilns, Sumida River, Suijin Shrine, Mount Tsukuba
remarks: Similar area as no. 35, 36

title: Dawn Inside the Yoshiwara (廓中東雲, Kakuchū shinonome)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Published two months prior to the reopening of Yoshiwara after it burnt down in the 1855 earthquake; Hiroshige may have submitted this design and title following the suicide of two courtesans and their two lovers at dawn of the 19th day of the 4th month of 1857

title: Distant View of Kinryūzan Temple and the Azuma Bridge (吾妻橋金龍山遠望, Azumabashi Kinryūzan enbo)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Sumida River, Azuma Bridge, Kinryūzan Temple, Mount Fuji

title: Bashō's Hermitage on Camellia Hill beside the Aqueduct at Sekiguchi (せき口上水端はせを庵椿やま, Sekiguchi jōsuibata Bashōan Tsubakiyama)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: Hachiman Shrine in Ichigaya (市ヶ谷八幡, Ichigaya Hachiman)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Ichigaya moat, shopping street, Hachiman Shrine
remarks: Dated one month after Hiroshige's death and therefore attributed to Hiroshige II by some art historians

title: Cherry Blossoms on the Banks of the Tama River (玉川堤の花, Tamagawa tsutsumi no hana)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Actually, the Tama River-Jōsui channel is depicted, with cherry trees far outside Edo
remarks: One of the first five prints sanctioned by the censors

title: Nihonbashi Bridge and Edobashi Bridge (日本橋江戸ばし, Nihonbashi Edobashi)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Fish barrel with first bonito of the season represents early summer

title: View of Nihonbashi itchōme Street (日本橋通一丁目略図, Nihonbashi Tōri itchōme ryakuzu)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Shirokiya shop on the right developed into one of the city's largest department stores and finally became part of the Tokyu Group

title: Yatsumi Bridge (八ツ見のはし, Yatsumi no hashi)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Yatsumi Bridge, Edo Castle, Mount Fuji

title: Yoroi Ferry, Koami-chō (鎧の渡し小網町, Yoroi no watashi Koami-chō)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: Seidō and Kanda River from Shōhei Bridge (昌平橋聖堂神田川, Shōheibashi Seidō Kandagawa)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Shōhei Bridge, Shōheizaka hill, Kanda River, wall of Yushima Seidō
remarks: Exaggerated size of hill on left

title: Suidō Bridge and the Surugadai Quarter (水道橋駿河台, Suidōbashi Surugadai)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: View from Surugadai, Kanda River, Suidō Bridge, Edo Castle, Mount Fuji
remarks: Koinobori carp streamers place the scene around the Boy's festival on the 5th day of the 5th month, the same month as the print was approved by the censors

title: Fudō Falls in Ōji (王子不動之滝, Ōji Fudō no taki)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Hasegawa Settan

title: Kumano Jūnisha Shrine at Tsunohazu, known as "Jūnisō" (角筈熊野十二社 俗称十二そう, Tsunohazu Kumano Jūnisha zokushō Jūnisō)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: The Sannō  Festival Procession at Kōjimachi itchōme (糀町一丁目山王祭ねり込, Kōjimachi itchōme Sannō Matsuri nerikomi)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Moat and Hanzōmon Gate of Edo Castle
remarks: Sannō  Festival was held one month prior to the approval of the print

title: The Paulownia Garden at Akasaka (赤坂桐畑, Akasaka Kiribatake)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Paulownia Garden, Tameike Pond, Hie Shrine, residence of daimyō Kuroda from Fukuoka
remarks: Hie Shrine is location for the Sannō Festival (see no. 51)

title: The Pagoda of Zōjōji Temple and Akabane (増上寺塔赤羽根, Zōjōjitō Akabane)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Zōjō-ji, Akabane Bridge, residence of daimyō Arima from Kurume

title: The Benkei Moat from Soto-Sakurada to Kōjimachi (外桜田弁慶堀糀町, Soto-Sakurada Benkeibori Kōjimachi)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: South-west embankment and moat of Edo Castle, residence of daimyō Ii from the Hikone Domain, Kōjimachi watchtower
remarks: Kōjimachi watchtower had been restored two months prior to publication after burning down in the 1855 earthquake

title: The Sumiyoshi Festival at Tsukudajima (佃しま住吉の祭, Tsukudajima Sumiyoshi no matsuri)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: The Sumiyoshi Festival is held on the 29th of the sixth month, i.e., in the month prior to publication of this print; writer of the inscription is identified on the banner as the creator of the table of contents of this series, Seikengū Gengyo

title: Mannen Bridge in Fukagawa (深川萬年橋, Fukagawa Mannenbashi)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: A turtle hanging from tub's handle use for Life release.

title: Mitsumata Wakarenofuchi (みつまたわかれの淵)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Reed-covered sandbank alludes to the artificial Nakazu island, location of a famous 18th-century pleasure district that was demolished in the course of the Kansei Reforms

title: Sudden Shower over Shin-Ōhashi bridge and Atake (大はしあたけの夕立, Ōhashi atake no yūdachi)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Shin-Ōhashi bridge, Sumida River, Atake district
remarks: Considered to be a masterpiece and possibly Hiroshige's most famous work; copied by Vincent van Gogh

title: Ryōgoku Bridge and the Great Riverbank (両国橋大川ばた, Ryōgokubashi Ōkawabata)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: At the time Japan's largest bridge

title: Asakusa River, Miyato River, Great Riverbank (浅草川大川端宮戸川, Asakusagawa Ōkawabata Miyatogawa)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Depicts pilgrimage to Mount Ōyama, the pilgrim's return from the mountain is shown in no. 76; names in title are synonymous names for the depicted section of the Sumida River; in later prints, the title was simplified to: In Boats at Ryōgoku with a distant View of Asakusa

title: The "Pine of Success" and Oumayagashi on the Asakusa River (浅草川首尾の松御厩河岸, Asakusagawa shubi no matsu Oumayagashi)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Hiroshige's "Illustrated Souvenirs of Edo" contains a mirror image of this view with  a woman standing upright in the boat

title: Komakata Hall and Azuma Bridge (駒形堂吾嬬橋, Komakatadō Azumabashi)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Komakata Hall, Sumida River, Azuma Bridge, Asakusa district
remarks: Lesser cuckoo (hototogisu) and Komakata Hall in the print allude to a 17th-century love poem attributed to Takao II, courtesan and lover of Date Tsunamune: Are you now, my love, near Komakata? Cry of the cuckoo!"

title: The Ayase River and Kanegafuchi (綾瀬川鐘か淵, Ayasegawa Kanegafuchi)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Northernmost point of the Sumida river depicted in this series

title: Horikiri Iris Garden (堀切の花菖蒲, Horikiri no hanashōbu)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Influenced European Art Nouveau

title: Inside Kameido Tenjin Shrine (亀戸天神境内, Kameidō Tenjin keidai)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Drum bridge and garden at Kameido Tenjin Shrine
remarks: Flowering wisteria indicates summer season

title: The Sazaidō Hall at the Five Hundred Rakan Temple (五百羅漢さゞゐ堂, Gohyaku Rakan Sazaidō)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: The Ferry at Sakasai (逆井のわたし, Sasakai no watashi)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: Open Garden at the Hachiman Shrine in Fukagawa (深川八まん山ひらき, Fukagawa Hachiman yamahiraki)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Print shows both azaleas and cherry trees in blossom which is unnatural for Edo; hill in the back is one of many miniature Mount Fuji that were scattered all over Edo

title: Sanjusangendō Hall in Fukagawa (深川三十三間堂, Fukagawa Sanjusangendō)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: About half of the hall is depicted

title: The mouth of the Nakagawa River (中川口, Nakagawaguchi)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Onagigawa Canal, Nakagawa River, Shinkawa Canal
remarks: Nakagawa River is the broad waterway in the middle running left-right

title: Scattered Pines on the Tone River (利根川ばらばらまつ, Tonegawa barabara-matsu)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: The Ferry at Haneda and the Benten Shrine (はねたのわたし弁天の社, Haneda no watashi Benten no yashiro)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Tama River, Benten Shrine, Edo Bay
remarks: The ground of the Benten Shrine is now occupied by Haneda Airport

title: The City Flourishing, the Tanabata Festival (市中繁栄七夕祭, Shichū han'ei Tanabata matsuri)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Arguably shows the view from Hiroshige's house; only print in series without a place name in the title

title: Silk Shops in Ōdenma-chō (大伝馬町ごふく店, Ōdenma-chō gofukudana)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: People depicted are carpenters

title: The Dyers' Quarter in Kanda (神田紺屋町, Kanda konya-chō)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Kanda dye works, Edo Castle, Mount Fuji
remarks: Kanji on first two white-blue fabrics reads "sakana" and is a reference to the publisher Sakanaya Eikichi, last two white-blue fabrics contain Hiroshige's monogram

title: Bamboo Quay by Kyōbashi Bridge (京橋竹がし, Kyōbashi Takegashi)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: Inari Bridge and the Minato Shrine in Teppōzu (鉄砲洲稲荷橋湊神社, Teppōzu Inaribashi Minato jinja)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Hatchōbori Canal, Minato Shrine, Inari Bridge, Mount Fuji

title: Teppōzu and Tsukiji Monzeki Temple (鉄砲洲築地門跡, Teppōzu Tsukiji Monzeki)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Like no. 79, this print has the changed series title: Entertaining Supplements to the One Hundred Famous Views of Edo, as likely Hiroshige wanted to end this series having produced 110 plates already at the time; he resumed the original title on the publisher insisting to do so

title: Shiba Shinmei Shrine and Zōjōji Temple (芝神明増上寺, Shiba Shinmei Zōjōji)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: Kanasugi Bridge and Shibaura (金杉橋芝浦, Kanasugibashi Shibaura)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Print shows pilgrims of the Nichiren sect; brown and white cloths at bottom left contain abbreviation "Uoei" of the publisher's name Sakanaya Eikichi

title: Ushimachi in Takanawa (高輪うしまち, Takanawa ushimachi)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Place name Takanawa meaning "high wheel" is symbolized by the wheel on the right and the rainbow

title: Moon Viewing (月の岬, Tsuki no Misaki)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Approximately same view as no. 83

title: Shinagawa Susaki (品川すさき)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Meguro River, Benten Shrine, Edo Bay with daiba
remarks: Establishment depicted in no. 82 might be the one cropped in the bottom left of this print

title: "Grandpa's Teahouse" in Meguro (目黒爺々が茶屋, Meguro jijigachaya)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: Kinokuni Hill and Distant View of Akasaka and the Tameike Pond (紀ノ国坂赤坂溜池遠景, Kinokunizaka Akasaka Tameike enkei)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: outer Benkeibori moat of Edo Castle
remarks: People depicted are samurai

title: Naitō Shinjuku in Yotsuya (四ッ谷内藤新宿, Yotsuya Naitō Shinjuku)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Naitō Shinjuku (first station of the Kōshū Kaidō)
remarks: Depiction of horse dung was criticized for being vulgar; Hiroshige likely alludes to the quote: "flowers thriving on the horse dung of Yotsuya" from the 1775 book Master Flashgold's Splendiferous Dream referring to the prostitutes of Shinjuku

title: Benten Shrine at the Inokashira Pond (井の頭の池弁天の社, Inokashira no ike Benten no yashiro)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Depicts most western point of the series and location furthest away from central Edo

title: Takinogawa in Ōji (王子滝の川, Ōji Takinogawa)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Takinogawa, Matsubashi Benten Shrine, Kongo-ji Temple
remarks: Original orange color of autumn color has turned brownish-black due to oxidation over time

title: "Moon Pine" in Ueno (上野山内月のまつ, Ueno sannai Tsuki no matsu)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Moon Pine, Shinobazu Pond, Benten Shrine

title: Night View of Saruwaka-machi (猿わか町よるの景, Saruwaka-machi yoru no kei)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Notable for the use of peoples' shadows giving the impression of puppets on a stage; the street was home to the Kabuki and marionette theatres

title: In the Akiba Shrine at Ukeji (請地秋葉の境内, Ukechi Akiba no keinai)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: People depicted in the bottom left might be Hiroshige as a monk with painting utensils, his wife Yasu and their adopted daughter Tatsu according to Henry D. Smith

title: Mokuboji Temple and Vegetable Fields on Uchigawa Inlet (木母寺内川御前栽畑, Mokubōji Uchigawa Gozensaibata)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Uchigawa inlet of Sumida River, Uekiya Han'emon restaurant
remarks: The Mokuboji temple mentioned in the title is close but not depicted in this print; Tokugawa Iesada had visited the restaurant one month before publication of the print

title: Niijuku Ferry (にい宿のわたし, Niijuku no watashi)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Northeasternmost scene of the series confirmed by detailed depiction of the Edo side of the river and vagueness on the opposite bank

title: The Maple Trees at Mama, the Tekona Shrine and Tsugihashi Bridge (真間の紅葉手古那の社継はし, Mama no momiji Tekona no yashiro Tsugihashi)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Edogawa River, Tekona Shrine, Tsugihashi Bridge
remarks: Original orange color of autumn color has turned brownish-black due to oxidation over time

title: View of Kōnodai and the Tone River (鴻の台とね川風景, Kōnodai Tonegawa Fūkei)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: Horie and Nekozane (堀江ねこざね, Horie Nekozane)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: branch of the Edogawa River, Horie and Nekozane villages, Edo Bay, Mount Fuji
remarks: One of the first five prints sanctioned by the censors

title: "Five Pines" and the Onagi Canal (小奈木川五本まつ, Ongagigawa Gohonmatsu)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Tree depicted is the only surviving of five pines and was at the time of Hiroshige known as "Five Pines"; Onagi Canal was in fact straight

title: Fireworks by Ryōgoku Bridge (両国花火, Ryōgoku hanabi)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: Kinryūzan Temple in Asakusa (浅草金龍山, Asakusa Kinryūzan)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Kaminarimon, Hōzōmon and pagoda of Sensō-ji
remarks: Thought to commemorate the restoration of the pagoda two month prior to the publication of the print after damage suffered in the 1855 earthquake

title: Nihon Embankment and Yoshiwara (よし原日本堤, Yoshiwara Nihondzutsumi)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: Asakusa Ricefields and Torinomachi Festival (浅草田甫酉の町詣, Asakusa tanbo Torinomachi mōde)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Yoshiwara teahouse, Chōkoku-ji temple (Ōtori Shrine), Mount Fuji
remarks: White cat represents courtesan who just finished her work; the festival of the rooster is hinted at by bear's paw hairpins resembling the good luck rakes carried by pilgrims in the background

title: Minowa, Kanasugi and Mikawashima (蓑輪金杉三河しま, Minowa Kanasugi Mikawashima)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: Senju Great Bridge (千住の大はし, Senju no ōhashi)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: One of the first five prints sanctioned by the censors

title: Koume Embankment (小梅堤, Koumetsutsumi)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: Oumayagashi (御厩河岸)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: The two women are yotaka (night hawks), the lowest class of prostitutes whose faces were often disfigured forcing them to apply very thick make-up

title: The Fukagawa Lumberyards (深川木場, Fukagawa kiba)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Character for "sakana" on the umbrella refers to Sakanaya Eikichi, the publisher of the series

title: Fukagawa Susaki and Jūmantsubo (深川州崎十万坪, Fukagawa Susaki Jūmantsubo)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: View of Shiba Coast (芝うらの風景, Shibaura no fūkei)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: One of the first five prints sanctioned by the censors

title: Minami Shinagawa and Samezu Coast (南品川鮫洲海岸, Minamishinagawa Samezu kaigan)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Edo Bay, Kaian-ji temple, Mount Tsukuba
remarks: Fishermen gather seaweed from submerged tree trunks

title: "Robe-Hanging Pine" at Senzoku no ike (千束の池袈裟懸松, Senzoku no ike Kesakakematsu)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Senzoku no ike, "Robe-Hanging Pine", Hachiman Shrine
remarks: One of the first five prints sanctioned by the censors; Nichiren is said to have hung his monk's robe on this pine while resting

title: Meguro Drum Bridge and Sunset Hill (目黒太鼓橋夕日の岡, Meguro taikobashi Yūhi no oka)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Publication date of this and three other prints (no. 24, 25, 84) depicting Meguro coincides with the exhibition of a Fudō Myōō icon

title: Atagoshita and Yabu Lane (愛宕下薮小路, Atagoshita Yabukōji)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Yabu Lane, residences of the daimyōs Katō from Minakuchi and Hijikata from Komono, gate of Atago Shrine
remarks: Yabu Lane is to the far right beyond the bamboo thicket lining it

title: Aoi Slope outside Toranomon gate (虎の門外あふひ坂, Toranomon soto Aoizaka)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: Bikuni Bridge in Snow (びくにはし雪中, Bikunihashi setchū)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Bikuni Bridge, outer moat of Edo Castle
remarks: Believed to be created by Hiroshige II based on empty foreground, depiction of wall and schematic arrangement of snowflakes. Sign on left advertises  "mountain whale" meat, referring to wild boar.

title: The Takata Riding Grounds (高田の馬場, Takada no baba)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: Sugatami Bridge, Omokage Bridge and Jariba at Takata (高田姿見のはし俤の橋砂利場, Takata Sugatami no hashi Omokage no hashi Jariba)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Kanda River, Omokage Bridge, Hikawa rice field, Hikawa Shrine, Sugatami Bridge
remarks: Notable for striking colors; Tokugawa Iesada crossed the Omokage Bridge in the publication month

title: View from the Hilltop of Yushima Tenjin Shrine (湯しま天神坂上眺望, Yushima Tenjin sakaue chōbō)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
depicted: Yushima Tenjin Shrine, Shinobazu Pond, Kan'ei-ji

title: Kitsunebi on New Year's Night under the Enoki Tree near Ōji (王子装束ゑの木大晦日の狐火, Ōji shōzoku wenoki ōtsugomorihi no kitsunebi)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo

title: View of the Paulownia Imperiales Trees at Akasaka on a Rainy Evening (赤坂桐畑雨中夕けい, Akasaka kiribatake uchū yūkei)
series: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo
remarks: Work by Hiroshige II; not always included in collections of One Hundred Famous Views of Edo; variously thought to be replacement print for no. 52, The Paulownia Garden at Akasaka, although its woodblock prints show significant wear indicating age. Alternatively a work on commission by Sakanaya Eikichi, celebrating Hiroshige II's adoption of his name and Hiroshige's seal
